**Competition**

February 5, 2025 

Edison Auditorium

***The Team*** 

Albert Napoli  

anapoli@marshall.usc.edu

3

www.pccsbdc.org 

2 

***Timeline*** 

• Submission of additional information – February 15th at 11:59 pm • Not everyone will advance to the quarterfinals • Quarterfinal Round on February 27th from 6:30 to 9:30 pm • 80 to 90 teams will present   
• 16 teams will advance   
• 5 Mandatory Training Sessions   
• March 1, 8, 15, 29, and April 5    
• Semifinal on April 10th at 6:30 pm   
• Final Round – April 25th from 9 am to 12 pm   
• Only 6 teams will present   
• Winner announced on May 1st at the Marcia Israel Awards Dinner 4

1   
2/6/25 

***Judging*** 

• Three judges per room   
• 5 Minute Pitch   
• 5 Minute Q & A   
• Judges are:   
• Angel and VC Investors   
• Professionals with M & A Experience   
• Serial Entrepreneurs   
• All presentations will be in person with very, very few exceptions 5 

***Mandatory Training*** 

• 15 finalists will participate in 6 training sessions in March and April  • During the sessions, the finalists will   
• Perform customer discovery   
• Hone their business models   
• Be trained on the Business Model Canvas   
• Learn how to pitch to investors   
• Be given individual and group mentoring   
• ***Attendance at all training sessions are mandatory***   
~~•~~ At least one member of the team must attend each session. 

7   
***Judging Criteria*** 

• Not solely about the pitch   
• Is it a valid plan and concept?   
• Who has the greatest chance of long-term success. • Score Sheet will be sent to everyone. 

6 

***Prizes*** 

***![][image3]***8

2   
2/6/25 

• New Venture Seed Competition 

• Platinum Prize \- $ 35,000   
• Gold Prize \- $ 20,000 (Victoria L. Wilson    
Memorial Award)   
• Two Silver Prizes \- $ 15,000 each   
• Two Bronze Prizes \- $ 10,000 each (John    
Nehori Award)   
• Helena of Troy Award- $ 10,000 Female Founder   
• Rebuild L.A. Award \- $ 10,000   
• People’s Choice Award – $ 1,000 • Voted on by the public for their favorite team. 

9   
Prizes Totaling Over  

$ 150,000 

• Concept Prize   
• Awarded to teams who are at the very    
beginning of bringing their idea to    
fruition   
• First Place \- $ 7,500   
• Second Prize – $ 5,000   
• Third Prize \- $ 2,500   
• Semifinal Competitors   
• Every team that competes in the semi final on April 10, 2025 will be awarded $ 1,500 (Lowenstein Foundation) 

10   
Prizes Totaling Over  

$ 150,000 

• Current Students – Scholarship   
• If you receive financial aid, we will pay to have    
an LLC or corporation set up to receive the    
money.   
• If you do not receive financial aid, the amount    
will be applied to your tuition. If there is a zero    
balance for tuition, then you will receive the    
prize money as a refund.   
• Alumni & Faculty \- Check

of stories, it would never be    
forgotten.”

2/6/25 

A Few Tips  

*for a great presentation* 

25 

Less \> More 27 

Avoid Too Much Data 

26 

Pique Their Interest 

28

7   
2/6/25 

Engage Your Audience 

29 

No MSU 

31   
Connect Emotionally 30 

What is Your  

Competitive  

Advantage? 

32

2/6/25 

Use Powerful Slides 33 

Answer the Question 

35

And Then Practice Some More 

34

9   
2/6/25 

Have Fun\! Show Passion\! 37 

Entrepreneurial Model 

Pain/ 

Your Investor Pitch 

*what to say* 

38 

Content of a Typical Pitch   
**Intro** Define the company/business in a single declarative sentence **Story** Authentic and relatable   
**Problem** Describe customer’s pain and how he/she addresses it today   
Unmet Need Product/   
Distribution 

Benefits Delivered   
**Converging Trends** Set up evolution of category and recent trends that make your solution  possible. (Why Now)   
**Solution** Demonstrate your company’s value proposition to make customer’s life better **Target Market** Identify the customer segment(s) you cater to   
**Market Size** Calculate the TAM, SAM & SOM   
Service Customer 

Revenue Model 

39   
**Competition/Differentiation** List competitors and highlight your competitive advantages **Economics** Provide unit economics   
**Team** Founders and management, advisors and board of directors **Traction/Next Steps** Show progress you made to date and your next steps **Social Responsibility** If your company has a social impact, what is it.   
**Contact** How do we get a hold of you   
**Sneaky Slides** Appendix with information 

40

10   
2/6/25 

***Slide Deck*** 

**Maximum of 13 slides for**  

**the judges review and**  

**approval** 

If slide deck is approved, you will be  

allowed to add “sneaky slides” prior  

to your presentation. 

41 

***What’s the Story?*** 

• Tell a story about your target customer(s) 

• The story may be about a problem you have  experienced. 

• Make it relatable to your audience 

43   
***Introductory Slide*** 

• Present your logo, company name 

• Include presenter’s names, job titles and contact  information 

• Include a picture of your product or service offering (if  you have one) 

42 

***What’s the Problem?*** 

• Describe the problem you are looking to address in simple,  clear concise terms 

• Current state, seriousness of problem    
• Desired future state, benefit to customer   
• Scale of the initial market 

44

11   
2/6/25 

***What’s Your Team’s Solution?*** 

• Describe your solution in simple, clear, concise terms.  • Key benefits, features for customers 

• Product design template   
• Timeline to develop design   
• Should not be “magical” technology 

45 

***The Business Model*** 

• Clearly and concisely explain how you are going to make  money 

• What’s the value to the customer?    
• ***Remember, it’s all about the customer\!*** 

47   
***Secret Sauce*** 

• What’s the proprietary, underlying “magic” that gives your  teams solution a clear, defensible advantage? 

• Design improvements over current offerings    
• What are you going to do particularly well that it will be difficult  to copy? 

46 

***Market Overview*** 

• What is the market size and prospective growth potential? • Who is your target customer? 

• What is your product’s positioning within the market? • Are there any market trends that you are leveraging? • What other possible applications are there for your products? 

48

12   
2/6/25 

***Market Size*** 

TAM 

SAM 

SOM 

49 

***Serviceable Available Market*** 

• Subset of TAM   
• Targeted segment that would purchase your products and services • Within your geographic reach   
• Needs to be in dollars 

51   
***Total Addressable Market*** 

• Total value of buyer/seller relationships in a market   
• Measured at point of consumption   
• Includes the entire value chain   
• Can be measured in units (people, locations, room nights, etc.)   
• Very broad number   
• Easiest to estimate   
• Needs to be in dollars 

50 

***Serviceable Obtainable Market*** 

• Subset of SAM   
• Also known as Target Market   
• Portion of the market that you can capture within a certain period of  time.   
• Needs to be in dollars 

52

13   
2/6/25 

***Marketing and Selling*** 

• How will you reach your customers   
• 1 or 2 marketing strategies   
• Sales cycle & strategy for initial market   
• How will you get your first customer? 

53 

***Competition*** 

• There is ALWAYS competition   
• If there isn’t, there’s probably not a market   
• Provide a competition analysis \- table/chart comparing your  solution to the current approach and major competitors • Comparison is based on key benefits   
• The more realistic you are, the more believable your case 55   
***Financial*** 

• How will you develop product/service?   
• Cost to develop and produce   
• Potential sales price to your customer   
• Competitive pricing analysis    
• Design Cost versus Customer Price   
• Cost/Margin calculation  

54 

***Team*** 

• List all the team members   
• Picture   
• What skills, resources, experience, or knowledge do they bring to the  team?   
• Board of Advisors can be key   
• What positions are you recruiting   
• “Do you bet on the horse or on the jockey?” 

56

14   
2/6/25 

***Social Impact*** 

• What is the impact of your product or service on the  environment? 

• Will your venture have a positive social impact?   
• Describe the values of your company   
• Will this be a major shift for good in the marketplace? 57 

***Sneaky Slides*** 

• Your team may choose to have some pre-prepared slides to  anticipate questions that you might get from the judges • Only 1 topic per slide 

• Keep them simple   
• Closing slide will be on screen for most of Q\&A, prepare  accordingly 

59 

***Conclusion*** 

• Wrap it all up   
• What are the next steps?   
• What information do you need to move forward? • Thank the judges 

• Open up for further questions 

58 

***What is the Purpose of the  Presentation?*** 

How you will efficiently use your  prize money to further your  

company, product or service. 

60

15   
2/6/25 

***Required Submission*** 

• By February 15th at 11:59 pm you ***MUST*** submit all of the  following or be eliminated   
• Slide Deck   
• Financial Statement   
• Two page write up 

61 

***Financial Statements*** 

• Three year profit and loss statement   
• It’s alright if you have a loss for a while   
• It should have some level of detail   
• Methodology behind the numbers   
• Be aggressive but realistic   
• Summary on the slides, detail on the “Sneaky Slide” 

63   
***Pro Tips*** 

• No Phantom Technology   
• USC students as your primary customers   
• Partnering with USC   
• Do you know how your technology works (blockchain,  algorithms, LLM’s)? 

• It’s simple and no one has done it before\!\!   
• No original ideas 

62 

***Two-Page Summary*** 

• You will answer six questions:   
• In two or three sentences, please explain your product or service in  laypersons terms.   
• What is the pain or unmet need in the market and your solution? • Prove that there is a pain or unmet need through research   
• Do NOT use terms like “I think” without data backing it up   
• Who is your target market and why?   
• Must be based on data 

64

16   
2/6/25 

***Two-Page Summary*** 

• What is your competitive advantage?   
• This must be unique and hard to replicate   
• Examples of what is not unique and easy to replicate   
• “We’re really motivated”   
• “We have a passion for the industry”   
• “We are USC students”   
• What is your market validation?   
• Customer discovery   
• Similar products   
• Extrapolation 

65 

***What will you receive tonight?*** 

• Link to second round   
• Copy of the slide decks   
• Proforma Template   
• Contact information for SBDC and Blackstone LaunchPad • Scoring sheet   
***Two-Page Summary*** 

• What is your go-to-market strategy?   
• Who is your initial customer(s) and why?   
• What marketing tactics will you use? 

66 

67 68

17
